front: Performer: in 1955 became the first African American to sing at the Metropolitan Opera, performing Ulrica in Verdi's Un ballo in maschera

Performer: American contralto; gave a landmark outdoor concert at the Lincoln Memorial on Easter Sunday, 1939 after the Daughters of the American Revolution refused to let her sing at Constitution Hall

Performer: Eleanor Roosevelt resigned from the DAR in protest over her exclusion from Constitution Hall; Interior Secretary Harold Ickes arranged the Lincoln Memorial concert (declaring 'all of us are free')
Performer: Horace Pippin painted her portrait after the Lincoln Memorial concert; Beauford Delaney painted her portrait against a yellow background
Performer: made a celebrated recording of Brahms's Two Songs for Voice, Viola, and Piano with violist William Primrose and pianist Franz Rupp
